title: Support NBA on automatic variables in tasks/functions with --timing
body:
## Summary

Non-blocking assignments (NBA) on automatic variables declared inside tasks and functions are rejected with an `INITIALDLY` error. IEEE 1800-2023 §6.21 only prohibits NBA on module-level automatic variables — task/function-local automatic variables should be legal NBA targets.

This pattern is commonly used in UVM (`wait_for_nba_region()`). Note that #6859 fixed a related but different issue — the task there used `static` variables (no INITIALDLY error), and the bug was in V3Timing temp variable scoping. This issue is about `automatic` variables being incorrectly rejected at the V3WidthCommit stage.

## Current Behavior

- NBA on an automatic variable inside a task/function produces: `%Error-INITIALDLY: Automatic lifetime variable 'xxx' in non-suspendable initial/final block`
- `initial` blocks containing timing controls (via inlined tasks) are not recognized as suspendable, so NBAs are converted to blocking assignments even with `--timing`

## Expected Behavior

- NBA on automatic variables inside tasks/functions should compile and simulate correctly with `--timing`
- `initial` blocks containing timing controls should preserve NBA semantics when `--timing` is enabled

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#80

---
PR is coming.
